paragraph: I sold the company to acme-co today. The deal closed at 9am. I'll spend the next year as an advisor before stepping away completely.
tier_rationale: life event + financial decision + relationship-status change with the company

paragraph: alice-example told me she's leaving the firm. She's giving notice tomorrow and starting at widget-co in two weeks.
tier_rationale: major commitment + relationship-status change in someone close to me

paragraph: I'm taking three months off starting next week. Mental health break. Doctor said I needed to or I'd burn out hard.
My therapist diagnosed me with anxiety today. Starting CBT next Thursday. Finally putting a name on what I've been feeling for six months.
alice-example and I broke up last night. Twelve years. We're going to try mediation before deciding on the divorce.
My dad died this morning. The cancer came back in February and we knew it was coming but it still hit hard.
I gave up alcohol on January 1st and I'm sticking with it. Six months sober today. Sleeping better, working out more.
Just signed the term sheet for fund-a's Series A. $15M lead, $25M total round. We're now formally in deal mode for the next 8 weeks.
alice-example is having the baby. Due in November. We told the family last weekend.
I'm leaving acme-co at the end of the quarter. Told the CEO this morning. Don't have a next thing yet, just need space.
Hospitalized last night. Heart palpitations turned out to be a-fib. They got me stable and home by morning, but I'm following up with cardiology Tuesday.
Decided to give up the apartment lease and move closer to the office. Starting the search this weekend. Want to be in by April 1st.
alice-example asked me to be the godmother. I said yes. Christening is in October.
Promoted to VP of Engineering today. Effective Monday. Reports double, scope triples.
tier_rationale: major commitment, role change with multi-quarter implications

paragraph: I prefer dark roast coffee in the mornings, no sugar, no milk. Tartine's is fine but I really like the small place on 24th.
I think the FAANG model of unlimited PTO actually causes people to take less vacation. The data backs it up. We should switch to a use-it-or-lose-it minimum.
tier_rationale: belief / strong opinion that reveals character

paragraph: I don't drink coffee in the afternoon anymore. Found that even a 1pm cappuccino destroys my sleep. Switched to herbal tea after lunch.
My favorite running route is the loop around the park, exactly 5.2 miles. I do it three times a week.
I've come around to thinking that AI agents will replace most knowledge work in the next 5 years. Hard to say what the wedge use case is yet.
alice-example doesn't drink. She's been sober for over a decade. Important to remember when planning anything social.
tier_rationale: durable preference about a recurring contact

paragraph: I prefer email over slack for any conversation longer than three turns. It compounds the wrong way otherwise.
Strongly believe small talk at the start of meetings wastes time. The first 30 seconds set the tone for the next hour.
I take vitamin D every morning. Got tested last fall, was deficient, started supplementing. Don't skip days.
I'm vegetarian when I cook at home, but I eat meat when I travel. It's a sustainability framing, not an ethics one.
I never schedule meetings before 10am. Mornings are for deep work. People who push back on this aren't worth working with.
I think compensation transparency is overrated. Markets work because info is private. The HR-side argument is mostly therapy for managers.
Generally I avoid LinkedIn. Most of what's on it is performative and the few useful messages get drowned. Twitter and email are enough.
We ate at Tartine for breakfast. The bread was good. Walked around the neighborhood after.
Meeting starts at 2pm in conference room B. The link is in the calendar invite. Don't forget the slides.
Picked up dry cleaning, dropped off the dog at daycare, grabbed coffee on 17th. Quick errands morning.
alice-example is at SFO terminal 2. Flight lands at 4:35pm. We'll grab dinner at 7.
Pizza for dinner. Got the half-pepperoni-half-mushroom. Watched the game. Sox lost.
Train was late again. 20 minutes. The new schedule from January has been worse than the old one for our line.
Reset the wifi. Modem light was orange. Came back after 90 seconds. Internet is back.
alice-example texted to ask if I want to grab lunch tomorrow. Picked the place near the park. 12:30.
Forgot my charger at the office. Borrowed one from reception. Will swap back tomorrow.
Picked up groceries. Forgot the garlic. Will pick that up tomorrow when I'm out anyway.
Set up the new laptop at lunch today. Took 20 minutes. Slack and email are working, calendar synced too.
Q3 OKRs review pushed to Thursday. Same time, same room. Pre-read coming Wednesday afternoon.
Bumped the standup to 10am Mondays starting next week. Half the team has conflicts at 9.
